name: Spoon River Rolled Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the shortening and sugar together at medium speed until well blended.
2. Add eggs and flavorings, and beat at medium speed until creamy. Scrape down the bowl before and after adding eggs and flavorings.
3. Stir flour, nutmeg (if desired), baking powder, baking soda, and salt together to blend well. Add these ingredients along with sour cream to the creamy mixture.
4. Cover and refrigerate for 3 hours to overnight.
5. Return dough to room temperature.
6. Roll out on a lightly floured board until it is 1/4 inch thick.
7. Cut with a 2-1/2 round cutter or an equivalent (see Note below).
8. Place dough on cookie sheets that have been sprayed with pan spray or lined with aluminum foil.
9. Bake at 350°F for 8 to 10 minutes, or until cookies are lightly browned.
10. Remove from the oven and place on a wire rack to cool to room temperature.
Note: Nutritive values for these cookies are based on the use of a 2-1/2 round cutter and a weight of just over 1 ounce per cookie. You may use other cutters as long as the finished cookie weighs about 1 ounce, and you might even make a 2-ounce cookie and figure the nutritive value and exchanges as twice those for the smaller cookie. You may add flavorings other than vanilla and lemon; if nuts or raisins are added, however, you are altering the nutritional values and exchanges for each cookie.
Food Exchanges per serving: 1 STARCH/BREAD EXCHANGE + 1 FAT EXCHANGE
Calories: 108, Fat: 5g, Carbohydrates: 15g, Sodium: 54mg, Cholesterol: 18mg. Source: Desserts for Diabeties by Mabel Cavaiani, R.D.
